pattern: Partition a timeline into at most k+1 contiguous segments where each segment is sized to its own maximum, and minimize total slack with an interval-cost dynamic program.
hints: Within any stretch of time between two resizes the array size is constant, so it must equal the largest demand in that stretch; everything below it is wasted.
Precompute the waste of making the slice from index i to j a single segment, then choose where to place the resizes.

Let dp[i][c] be the least waste covering the prefix up to time i using c segments. Transition by trying every previous cut point and adding the precomputed segment waste; with k resizes you get up to k+1 segments.

edge_inputs.description: two times, no resizes allowed, single size covers both
one resize lets a tall final demand stand alone
valley in the middle exploited by two resizes
enough resizes to give every time its own size
already non-decreasing but no resizes forces the peak on everyone
all equal demands waste nothing regardless of k

stress.description: random demands with a random feasible resize budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 1000001) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(0, n)
    return [nums, k]
